summary: Ukraine launched its most massive UAV attack on Russian territory this year, targeting Moscow and Sevastopol with over 120 drones. Russia responded with a massive counter-launch of 287 Shahed-type drones against Ukrainian regions, resulting in significant air defense activity. The conflict is currently characterized by intensified long-range drone warfare and attrition in the rear areas of both nations.
key_points: Massive Ukrainian UAV strike on Moscow and Sevastopol
Russia launched 287 drones against Ukraine in one night
Ukrainian 'Peklo' jet-powered drone reported in Moscow suburbs
Intensified use of AI-driven 'Hornet' type drones by Ukraine
Russian air defense claimed 556 Ukrainian UAV interceptions
Ukrainian air defense intercepted 279 Russian UAVs
EU to allocate €5.9 billion specifically for Ukrainian drones

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine executed a record-breaking long-range UAV strike targeting the Moscow region and Sevastopol, demonstrating significant penetration of Russian airspace. Russia countered with a massive wave of 287 Shahed-type drones targeting various Ukrainian oblasts, including Dnipropetrovsk and Sumy. Both sides are increasingly relying on autonomous and jet-powered drone technology to strike deep into rear logistics and infrastructure.

points: Ukrainian forces utilized a new jet-powered long-range kamikaze drone named 'Peklo' to strike the Moscow suburb of Putylkovo (Source: @eRadarrua/92184).
The Armed Forces of Ukraine are deploying 'Hornet'-type UAVs equipped with American AI, capable of operating autonomously up to 150 km from the front lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
Ukrainian air defense units successfully intercepted and suppressed 279 enemy UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Italmas models, during a single night (Source: @kpszsu/62080).
The EU is expected to allocate €5.9 billion from its loan tranche specifically for the procurement of drones for the Ukrainian Armed Forces by mid-June (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30223).
Ukrainian helicopter pilots are currently undergoing advanced instructor training in the UK under Operation Interstorm to prepare for future fleet expansions (Source: @DeepStateUA/23510).
Russian forces launched a massive wave of 287 Shahed-type, Gerbera, and Italmas drones against Ukrainian territories, including Oryol, Kursk, and Bryansk (Source: @kpszsu/62080).
The Russian Ministry of Defense reported intercepting 556 Ukrainian UAVs over various Russian regions during a single night of operations (Source: @tass_agency/376230).
A significant Ukrainian UAV attack targeted the Moscow region, resulting in 12 injuries near the Moscow Refinery and damage to civilian infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
Russian sappers from the 'Sever' unit reported the neutralization of numerous Ukrainian UAVs and mines within the Kursk region (Source: @tass_agency/376202).
UAV debris was reported falling at Sheremetyevo Airport, impacting aviation operations (Source: @tass_agency/376224).
Russian military activity has intensified in the Krasnaya Liman sector, with ongoing battles for control of several settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
The operational tempo in the east is currently hindered by a 20-30 km 'dead zone' created by tactical UAV surveillance (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
Russian forces are attempting to leverage high-level reporting successes to claim territorial gains in the Krasnaya Liman area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
UAV activity was reported in the northern regions of Ukraine, including movements toward the Sumy and Chernihiv oblasts (Source: @eRadarrua/92140; @mon1tor_ua/66764).
Russian drone strikes were noted in the Oryol and Bryansk regions, indicating sustained pressure from the northern border (Source: @kpszsu/62080).
Ukrainian UAVs targeted Sevastopol, causing damage to power lines and civilian residential structures (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
A Ukrainian UAV was detected in the Black Sea towards the direction of Odesa (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66764).
Russian drone strikes were directed toward the southern regions, including Milleryovo and Primorsko-Akhtarsk (Source: @kpszsu/62080).
Ukraine is increasingly using AI-integrated drones that utilize target 'libraries' to strike logistics, such as KAMAZ trucks and fuel tankers in Zaporozhye (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
Russia's mass UAV deployment has reached record levels, utilizing multiple types of drones to overwhelm air defense systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
The use of jet-powered drones like the 'Peklo' represents a significant evolution in Ukrainian long-range strike capabilities (Source: @eRadarrua/92184).
Air defense effectiveness is being tested by 'imitation drones' (Parodiya) used by Russia to mask real attacks (Source: @kpszsu/62080).

key_developments: Ukraine launched a massive UAV strike on Moscow and Sevastopol, causing 12 injuries and infrastructure damage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
Russia deployed 287 Shahed-type and imitation drones in a single night against Ukrainian regions (Source: @kpszsu/62080).
The EU will dedicate €5.9 billion of its upcoming loan specifically to drone procurement for Ukraine (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30223).
Ukraine is deploying AI-driven 'Hornet' drones capable of autonomous strikes up to 150 km deep (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30225).
A jet-powered Ukrainian 'Peklo' drone successfully struck the Moscow suburb of Putylkovo (Source: @eRadarrua/92184).
Russian air defense claimed to have shot down 556 Ukrainian UAVs across Russian territory (Source: @tass_agency/376230).
Ukrainian helicopter pilots are undergoing specialized instructor training in the UK via Operation Interstorm (Source: @DeepStateUA/23510).
sections.threat_assessment: The threat level is extremely high due to the transition toward autonomous, AI-driven drone warfare. Both sides are successfully penetrating deep into the enemy's rear, targeting logistics, power grids, and command centers. The introduction of jet-powered UAVs and AI-target libraries suggests a shift toward high-speed, low-intercept-probability strikes that will challenge existing air defense architectures.
sections.regional_response: NATO members, particularly the UK, are increasing training support through programs like Operation Interstorm. The EU is pivoting its financial support toward high-tech drone procurement to counter Russian mass-launch tactics. Meanwhile, Russia is attempting to bolster its domestic air defense to mitigate the impact of increasingly sophisticated Ukrainian long-range strikes.
sections.intelligence_notes: The 'dead zone' of 20-30 km created by tactical UAVs is currently preventing significant operational breakthroughs on the ground. Intelligence suggests that the integration of American combat AI (e.g., Palantir) into Ukrainian drone systems is providing a significant technological edge in target identification. Monitoring the deployment of jet-powered drones is critical for assessing future strike depths.
